content.problemStatement:
CodeChef recently revamped its practice page to make it easier for users to identify the next problems they should solve by introducing some new features:


Recent Contest Problems - contains only problems from the last 2 contests
Separate Un-Attempted,  Attempted, and All tabs
Problem Difficulty Rating - the Recommended dropdown menu has various difficulty ranges so that you can attempt the problems most suited to your experience
Popular Topics and Tags
Our Chef is currently practicing on CodeChef and is a beginner. The count of ‘All Problems’ in the Beginner section is XXX. Our Chef has already ‘Attempted’ YYY problems among them. How many problems are yet ‘Un-attempted’?


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the count of 'All problems' in the Beginner's section and the count of Chef's 'Attempted' problems, respectively.


Output Format
Output a single integer in a single line — the number of problems that are yet 'Un-attempted'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the total number of problems in the Beginner section and the number of problems Chef has already attempted.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line — the number of problems that are still un‑attempted (X − Y).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 10‑4 = 6 un‑attempted problems.\nTest case 2: 10‑10 = 0.\nTest case 3: 1000‑990 = 10.
Only one problem exists and it has been attempted, so 0 remain.

solution.approach: For each test case we just compute the difference between the total number of problems X and the already attempted count Y. The answer is X‑Y. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        x = int(data[idx]); y = int(data[idx+1]); idx += 2
        out.append(str(x - y))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
